user_query:
Set up the two-layer SEO memory for our B2B SaaS project "Acme Analytics" and refresh it with the latest ranking check.

Read all inputs from input/ and write all outputs under output/.

Inputs to use (all in input/):
- current_date.txt — single line with YYYY-MM-DD; use this exact date for all "Last Updated" stamps and dated filenames
- hero_keywords.csv — columns: keyword,target_rank,volume
- secondary_keywords.csv — columns: keyword,target_rank,volume
- long_tail_keywords.csv — columns: keyword,target_rank,volume
- ranking_check.csv — columns: keyword,current_rank (latest ranks for keywords that appear in the keyword lists)
- competitors.csv — columns: domain,da,position_note
- metrics.json — object with fields for current, previous, target values; schema: { "Organic Traffic": {"current": N, "previous": N, "target": N}, ... }
- campaigns.yaml — list of campaigns with fields: name, start, end, goal, status, progress (0-100)
- promote.txt — newline-separated keywords to promote into the hot cache
- demote.txt — newline-separated keywords to demote out of the hot cache
- glossary_seed.md — starter content to merge into the project glossary

What to produce (all under output/):
1) Create the directory structure for the cold storage:
   - output/memory/
     - glossary.md
     - keywords/
       - hero-keywords.md
       - secondary-keywords.md
       - long-tail-keywords.md
       - historical-rankings.csv
     - competitors/
       - primary-competitors.md
       - analysis-history/
     - audits/
       - technical/
       - content/
       - domain/
       - backlink/
     - content-calendar/
       - active-calendar.md
       - published-content.md
       - archive/
     - reports/
       - monthly/
       - quarterly/
       - campaign/

2) Create and populate output/CLAUDE.md (the hot cache) with these sections and constraints:
   - Title: "Acme Analytics - SEO Memory (Hot Cache)"
   - "Last Updated": exactly the date from input/current_date.txt
   - Active Target Keywords section with two tables:
     a) Hero Keywords (Priority 1)
     b) Secondary Keywords (Priority 2)
     Fill rows using the keyword lists and latest ranks from ranking_check.csv. Include columns: Keyword | Current Rank | Target Rank | Volume | Status
     Status rule (deterministic):
       - achieved if current_rank <= target_rank
       - optimizing if current_rank <= target_rank + 3 and not achieved
       - tracking otherwise
   - Primary Competitors section listing each domain from competitors.csv with "(DA: X)" and a single-line position note
   - Current Optimization Priorities: add 2–4 succinct bullets derived from obvious gaps (e.g., keywords furthest from targets or competitor advantages in position_note); keep concise
   - Key Metrics Snapshot: a table with rows for each metric key in metrics.json, and columns: Metric | Current | Previous | Change | Target. Use the numbers from metrics.json (Change can be simple Current-Previous with sign; formatting is up to you)
   - Active Campaigns: one subsection per campaign in campaigns.yaml showing Duration, Goal, Status, and Progress X%
   - Quick Reference Notes: 2–3 bullets summarizing the most actionable next steps
   - Required cross-references (verbatim lines):
       - "Full keyword database: memory/keywords/"
       - "For project terminology, see: memory/glossary.md"
   - Keep the entire file under 150 lines

   Promotion/demotion in the hot cache:
   - Promote: Any keyword listed in input/promote.txt must appear in the hot cache (Hero or Secondary) regardless of which keyword file it originated from; include a note next to the keyword like "(Promoted [YYYY-MM-DD] - manual request)"
   - Demote: Any keyword listed in input/demote.txt must not appear anywhere in the hot cache tables (remove it if it would otherwise appear)
   - If a keyword appears in both promote.txt and demote.txt, treat it as promoted

3) Cold storage keyword files under output/memory/keywords/:
   - hero-keywords.md, secondary-keywords.md, long-tail-keywords.md: list the keywords with their target_rank and volume; include a short header with "Last Updated: {date}"
   - historical-rankings.csv: ensure a header "date,keyword,current_rank"; append one row per keyword present in hero_keywords.csv using the date from current_date.txt and the current_rank from ranking_check.csv (one row per keyword). If a hero keyword is missing from ranking_check.csv, omit that row.

4) Competitors files under output/memory/competitors/:
   - primary-competitors.md: a bullet list of the domain and DA for each competitor from competitors.csv
   - analysis-history/{YYYY-MM-DD}-analysis.md: create a dated summary file (date from current_date.txt) with a short paragraph summarizing competitor notes (you can synthesize a 3–5 sentence summary from the position_note fields)

5) Glossary under output/memory/glossary.md:
   - Start from glossary_seed.md and add a top header "Acme Analytics - SEO Glossary" and a "Last Updated: {date}" line; ensure it includes a small "Custom Segments" table with entries for Hero KWs, Quick Wins, and Brand Defense (fill reasonable counts)

Additional rules:
- Use only the input files listed above for data; do not invent ranks or DAs beyond what is provided
- Use input/current_date.txt for all date stamps and for dated filenames
- Ensure output/CLAUDE.md stays concise and readable (<=150 lines) while covering all required sections
- All paths must be written relative to the workspace under output/

Deliverables summary (must exist):
- output/CLAUDE.md
- output/memory/glossary.md
- output/memory/keywords/{hero-keywords.md,secondary-keywords.md,long-tail-keywords.md,historical-rankings.csv}
- output/memory/competitors/{primary-competitors.md,analysis-history/YYYY-MM-DD-analysis.md}
- plus the created empty directory structure for audits/, content-calendar/, and reports/
